title: Extend glossary + guides to a 2nd language pair
context: M1 requires the pipeline and glossaries to cover ≥2 language pairs so the project is not limited to a single translation target. The first language pair was established in glossary-001. This task builds a second glossary YAML using the same structure and schema, and ensures the plain-language guide and cultural-sensitivity guide reference any language-pair-specific considerations for the new target language. A qualified bilingual reviewer for the new language pair must sign off.
objective: Build an initial glossary YAML for a 2nd language pair (source language → second target language) with ≥40 entries, mirroring the structure of glossary-001: heritage/art-history terminology, proper nouns with preserve/transliterate conventions, a transliteration table where applicable, and a do-not-translate list. Update the plain-language guide and cultural-sensitivity guide with any language-pair-specific notes. Obtain qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off for the new language pair.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML file validated against glossarySchema from schema-001; contains ≥40 entries for a second, distinct language pair not covered by glossary-001
Each entry contains: term (source language), translation or preserve directive, part-of-speech, and notes for context or usage examples

Separate sections for: general heritage/art-history terminology, proper nouns with preserve/transliterate annotation, transliteration conventions (if applicable for the script pair), and do-not-translate list
Plain-language guide (guide-001) and cultural-sensitivity guide (guide-002) updated with any language-pair-specific plain-language targets or sensitivity considerations for the new target language

Reviewed and signed off by a qualified bilingual reviewer for the new language pair (native or near-native target language competence with heritage-translation experience); glossary version and sign-off recorded in the file header
output: glossaries/<src>-<tgt2>.yaml — initial glossary for the second language pair, validated against glossarySchema; updated guides/plain-language.md and guides/cultural-sensitivity.md if language-specific notes are needed
